decks.notes: This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy and controlling the game while also putting pressure on them with aggressive creatures. It utilizes powerful spells like Force of Will and Ancestral Recall to control the game, while also utilizing efficient creatures like Deathrite Shaman and Collector Ouphe to apply pressure. The deck also includes powerful mana acceleration in the form of Moxes and Black Lotus to help play multiple spells in a turn. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion adds additional value and utility to the deck.

This deck focuses on using fast mana acceleration and powerful artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. By combining artifact synergy with efficient mana sources, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence early in the game. The mix of creatures, artifacts, and planeswalkers allows for flexibility in how to approach different matchups, while the inclusion of disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle helps to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Ultimately, this deck aims to achieve a swift victory through proactive plays and efficient resource management.

This deck focuses on using disruptive creatures and powerful spells to control the game while applying pressure on the opponent. By utilizing cards like Collector Ouphe and Psychic Frog, it can disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining a consistent threat on the board. The efficient removal spells and counterspells help to protect its own creatures and maintain board dominance. The addition of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion offers additional value and recursion capabilities, ensuring a strong and resilient gameplay experience.

This deck focuses on using control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually establishing a board presence. It utilizes a variety of spells to counter threats, remove key creatures, and draw cards. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value from the graveyard. By efficiently managing resources and controlling the pace of the game, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through incremental advantage.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while setting up powerful combos and card advantage engines. Using counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation, alongside powerful tutors like Demonic Tutor and Vampiric Tutor, the deck aims to control the game tempo. With cards like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk for extra card advantage and extra turns, combined with powerful artifacts like Time Vault and Bolas's Citadel, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory in a Vintage 1 vs 1 matchup.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and mana acceleration to quickly deploy threats and disrupt opponents' strategies. The key strategy involves using cards like Grim Monolith and mana-producing artifacts to power out powerful threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Defense Grid to hinder opponents' game plans. By combining fast mana production with aggressive threats and disruptive elements, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents and secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on using various control elements such as counter spells and removal to disrupt opponents while setting up powerful combos with Gifts Ungiven. The deck also utilizes card advantage through cantrips and card draw spells to ensure consistency in its game plan. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a companion that allows for additional card recursion. The overall strategy revolves around efficiently managing resources, controlling the game's pace, and ultimately winning through powerful combinations and synergy between cards.

This deck aims to quickly assemble a combo involving artifacts such as Grim Monolith and The Fantasticar to generate a significant amount of mana. This mana is then used to cast cards like Paradoxical Outcome, Stock Up, and Sink into Stupor to draw through the deck and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of key cards like The One Ring and Manifold Key provide additional utility and win conditions. The deck also includes powerful cards like Force of Will and Ancestral Recall to protect its combo and maintain card advantage.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to cast Doomsday and set up a winning situation with Thassa's Oracle. The deck includes a variety of fast mana acceleration cards like Black Lotus and Moxen, along with powerful draw spells and cantrips to help dig through the deck and find the key pieces needed to win the game. The deck also includes disruption in the form of counterspells and discard spells to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The primary focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of countermagic, removal spells, and disruption. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a companion providing additional value by recurring cheap creatures. With a mix of powerful vintage staples like Force of Will, Ancestral Recall, and Time Walk, along with disruptive elements like Mindbreak Trap and Null Rod, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents, ultimately grinding them out with card advantage and efficient threats. The mana base is designed to support the deck's game plan with access to various colors and utility lands.

The deck focuses on fast and aggressive plays using artifact creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and disruption cards like Chalice of the Void. It also utilizes powerful artifacts like The Fantasticar and The One Ring to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Grim Monolith and generate card advantage with Sensei's Divining Top. Key cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional value and control options, while support cards like Manifold Key and Mystic Forge help to manipulate the game state in your favor. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of speed, disruption, and powerful artifacts.

This deck utilizes artifacts and powerful mana sources to quickly assemble a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats. By leveraging cards like Grim Monolith and Ancient Tomb for fast mana generation, along with powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Mystic Forge, the deck aims to establish a dominant position on the battlefield early in the game. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and threats, while cards like Sensei's Divining Top and Manifold Key help to maintain card advantage and maximize the deck's potential.

The deck is focused on disrupting and slowing down the opponent's plays through hate cards like Damping Sphere, Void Mirror, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Chancellor of the Annex. This allows the aggressive creatures like Seasoned Dungeoneer, Witch Enchanter, and Archon of Emeria to push through damage efficiently. The versatile mana base with Moxen and powerful lands like Ancient Tomb and Cavern of Souls allows for a consistent and explosive start. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure while hindering the opponent's game plan, creating a challenging board state for the opponent to navigate.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 Aggro - Hatebear deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategies with hate cards like Chalice of the Void, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, Void Mirror, and Chancellor of the Annex, while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Seasoned Dungeoneer and Archon of Emeria. The inclusion of mana acceleration from Moxen and Chrome Mox allows the deck to deploy threats quickly, and disruptive lands like Wasteland and Karakas help control the game. The deck aims to disrupt and overwhelm opponents with hate cards and aggressive creatures to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling mana-generating artifacts and utilizing the powerful draw spell Paradoxical Outcome to generate massive card advantage and gain a significant advantage in resources. By constantly bouncing back and replaying artifacts, the deck aims to generate a large amount of mana and draw into key combo pieces to set up a game-ending win condition. The inclusion of efficient counterspells and disruptive elements helps protect the combo pieces and ensure the opponent has a difficult time disrupting its game plan.

The deck focuses on generating card advantage and combo potential through the use of cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Thundertrap Trainer. It utilizes powerful mana acceleration from artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen to cast spells quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Force of Will and Mindbreak Trap provide disruption against opponent's strategies, while Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a valuable companion for additional recursion of key cards. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with overwhelming card advantage and powerful spells.

The deck focuses on assembling powerful artifact-based combos to generate fast and oppressive board states. The strategy revolves around utilizing Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Transmute Artifact to tutor for key pieces such as Time Vault, Bolas's Citadel, and Lórien Revealed. With a mix of disruptive spells like Force of Will and Mana Drain, the deck aims to control the game while building up towards a game-winning combo. Access to powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Ancestral Recall provides additional speed and card advantage, making this deck a formidable force in Vintage 1 vs 1 matches.

A deck focused on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption spells, utilizing the power of black, blue, and green cards. The deck aims to control the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and generating card advantage. It also includes a companion, Lurrus of the Dream-Den, to provide additional value throughout the game. The deck contains a diverse mix of powerful spells, mana acceleration with Moxen and rituals, along with a strong mana base to support its strategy.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful artifact combo using cards like Phyrexian Metamorph, Coveted Jewel, Grim Monolith, and The One Ring. It uses cards like Transmute Artifact and Tinker to fetch key pieces, while also running powerful mana acceleration like Black Lotus and Moxen. The deck aims to generate a large amount of mana quickly to play out powerful spells like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and Paradoxical Outcome. It also utilizes disruptive cards like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to control the game and protect its combo.

The deck revolves around assembling powerful artifacts such as Coveted Jewel, The One Ring, and Relic of Sauron to generate immense value and outpace the opponent. Utilizing cards like Transmute Artifact and Tinker to tutor for key pieces while controlling the board with counterspells like Force of Will. The deck also includes powerful mana acceleration with cards like Grim Monolith and Mishra's Workshop to quickly ramp into its threats. The overall strategy focuses on combo potential and overwhelming the opponent with a powerful artifact synergy.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through counterspells, removal, and hand disruption while generating card advantage through various sources like Snapcaster Mage, Ancestral Recall, and Treasure Cruise. The deck also includes powerful artifacts like Null Rod and Black Lotus to further control the game. Additionally, the inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value by allowing the player to cast cheaper cards from the graveyard. Overall, the deck aims to control the game until it can assemble a winning board state or combo off for the win.

The deck focuses on utilizing disruptive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frogs, alongside powerful control spells like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares, to keep the opponent's board in check. It also includes disruptive cards like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade and Pithing Needle to further disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also features key card draw and tutor spells like Ancestral Recall, Demonic Tutor, and Dig Through Time to ensure card advantage and consistency. The land base includes Wastelands to disrupt the opponent's mana base and utility lands like Urza's Saga for additional value.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan with counterspells and removal spells. It utilizes powerful cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to control the board and protect its own threats. The inclusion of companions like Lurrus of the Dream-Den provides additional value and consistency to the deck. By efficiently managing resources and leveraging its versatile card pool, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in a grindy matchup.

The deck focuses on fast mana acceleration and powerful artifacts to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It uses cards like Grim Monolith, Mana Crypt, and Moxen to ramp up mana production, allowing for early plays of threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The deck also includes key combo pieces like Time Vault and The One Ring to close out games swiftly. Defense Grid helps protect the deck from disruption, while tools like Pithing Needle and Portable Hole provide versatility in dealing with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to be proactive and aggressive in its strategy.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts, utilizing cards like Glaring Fleshraker and The One Ring. It also focuses on generating mana quickly through cards like Grim Monolith and mana-producing artifacts. The deck includes various key artifacts like Time Vault and The Fantasticar, along with support cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator. The strategy revolves around playing threats quickly and efficiently, establishing control over the game through a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts.

The deck focuses on quickly establishing aggressive board presence and utilizing artifacts to generate mana and card advantage. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and The Fantasticar, while also utilizing artifacts like Grim Monolith and Sensei's Divining Top for additional support. The inclusion of cards like The One Ring and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain add additional versatility and power to the deck. The deck also includes various artifact mana accelerants and disruptive spells to control the game and ultimately secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's plays and maximizing card advantage through a combination of countermagic, card draw, and graveyard recursion. The key strategy involves using Gifts Ungiven to tutor for specific cards to either disrupt the opponent's game plan or set up powerful combos. The deck also includes powerful mana acceleration in the form of artifact mana and land tutors. With Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion, the deck can continually recur key threats or disruption spells, making it difficult for the opponent to gain traction in the game.

This deck focuses on disrupting opponents with hate cards while aggressively attacking with creatures. The key strategy is to control the game by limiting the opponent's options with cards like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex, while also generating card advantage with Seasoned Dungeoneer and White Plume Adventurer. The deck also utilizes artifact ramp and disruption cards like Void Mirror to further control the game. Overall, the goal is to pressure the opponent while keeping them off balance with hate cards and disruptive elements.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using powerful spells and Oath of Druids to summon strong creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers. With a mix of counterspells, tutors, and disruptive cards, it aims to disrupt the opponent's plans while establishing its own board presence. The deck also includes cards like Black Lotus and Bolas's Citadel to generate mana and card advantage, allowing the player to stay ahead in the game. The combination of control elements and Oath of Druids makes this deck a formidable opponent in Vintage 1 vs 1 matches.

The main idea of this deck is to use the card Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers. By controlling the game with counterspells like Force of Will and interaction spells like Show and Tell, the deck can disrupt the opponent's game plan and overwhelm them with powerful creatures and card advantage. The deck also includes additional support cards like Oko, Thief of Crowns and Gaea's Blessing to further enhance its control strategy and ensure victory.

The deck revolves around utilizing cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Time Vault to generate an explosive amount of mana and draw cards, allowing the player to assemble a powerful combo quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Mana Crypt, Mana Vault, and Moxen accelerates the deck's strategy, while cards like Yawgmoth's Will and Demonic Tutor provide additional consistency and access to key combo pieces. With the ability to draw cards, generate mana, and protect key pieces with countermagic, the deck aims to quickly set up a game-winning sequence of plays.

The focus of this deck is to assemble a powerful synergy of fast mana acceleration, card draw, counterspells, and combo pieces to create game-winning plays. By utilizing efficient mana rocks and powerful card draw like Ancestral Recall, the deck aims to assemble the necessary combo pieces quickly while protecting itself with counterspells like Force of Will. With access to powerful combo finishers like Time Walk and Timetwister, the deck can quickly take control of the game and overwhelm opponents before they have a chance to react.
